title: Systemic uses of electric fields
abstract: An apparatus includes multiple first reservoirs and multiple second reservoirs joined with a substrate. Selected ones of the multiple first reservoirs include a reducing agent, and first reservoir surfaces of selected ones of the multiple first reservoirs are proximate to a first substrate surface. Selected ones of the multiple second reservoirs include an oxidizing agent, and second reservoir surfaces of selected ones of the multiple second reservoirs are proximate to the first substrate surface.
claims:
Claims 1. A system for treating bacterial antibiotic resistance comprising a substrate comprising one or more biocompatible electrodes configured to generate at least one of a low level electric field (LLEF) or low level electric current (LLEC). 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the biocompatible electrodes comprise a first array comprising a pattern of microcells formed from a first conductive material, and a second array comprising a pattern of microcells formed from a second conductive material. 3. The system of claim 2 wherein the first conductive material and the second conductive material comprise the same material. 4. The system of claim 3 wherein the first and second array each comprise a discrete circuit. 5. The system of claim 4, further comprising a power source. 6. The system of claim 2 wherein the first array and the second array spontaneously generate a LLEF. 7. The system of claim 6 wherein the first array and the second array spontaneously generate a LLEC when contacted with an electrolytic solution. 8. The system of claim 1 , wherein said first and second arrays comprise microcells on the interior of capillary tubing. 9. The system of claim 8, further comprising a pump. 10. A method for treating bacterial antibiotic resistance comprising applying a low level micro- current (LLEC) of between 1 and 200 micro-amperes to a tissue where said treatment is desired. 11. The method of claim 10 wherein applying comprises affixing a LLEC system comprising a pliable substrate comprising on its surface a multi-array matrix of biocompatible microcells. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein said multi-array matrix comprises: 

 a first array comprising a pattern of microcells comprising a conductive material; and a second array comprising a pattern of microcells comprising a conductive material, such arrays defining at least one voltaic cell for spontaneously generating at least one electrical current with the conductive material of the first array when said first and second arrays are introduced to an electrolytic solution. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein said applying a low level micro-current (LLEC) of between 1 and 200 micro-amperes to a tissue where said treatment is desired comprises passing a bodily fluid across a first array comprising a pattern of microcells comprising a conductive material; and a second array comprising a pattern of microcells comprising a conductive material, such arrays defining at least one voltaic cell for spontaneously generating at least one electrical current with the conductive material of the first array when said first and second arrays are introduced to an electrolytic solution on the interior of capillary tubing.
